prompt:
Enable the valley event bus on classic-laddie. the-valley's phase1 event log just merged (rev b5ae157): its NixOS module now has services.valley.bus options (see nix/valley-host.nix in the-valley) — a NATS JetStream bus, defaulting to disabled, listening on 127.0.0.1:4222 when enabled.

Task: in cosmo's classic-laddie configuration, find where services.valley is configured (the valley host module consumed from the the-valley flake input) and set services.valley.bus.enable = true. Add a short comment in the file's style noting the bus listens on localhost only, and that widening the listener is gated on bus authorization (the-valley .the-valley/bugs/bd-d853d9c-bus-unauthenticated.md).

Constraints:
- Do NOT set bus.listen — the localhost default is a deliberate security posture from the security review; enabling only.
- The flake.lock's the-valley input must already contain the bus module (rev b5ae157 or later, which includes commit 4a10664). If the lock on main is older — the auto-bump PR #651 may not have merged yet — bump the the-valley input in this PR too, or rebase once #651 lands.
- Verify before pushing: the classic-laddie NixOS configuration evaluates/builds with the option enabled (the repo's CI dry-runs builds; run the equivalent locally, e.g. building the classic-laddie toplevel or at minimum nix flake check as this repo's CI does).
- Open a normal PR. In the description, note it enables a new (localhost-only) service on the host and needs the operator's personal approval — do not mark it for auto-merge.
